content: Do they like not add radio drama to psyche deepdive?
it's in the archives
Why dont they just add it into the story
probably because it's not a direct part of the character's story most times
they're meant to be "extras" and "optional"

like take the 2.5.0 radio drama (arachne's) that details ||the last few moments of before nefraea was cast into the abyssal prison||, and while yes, it's possible to understand perfectly fine the whole a2c5 story without ever seeing it, the information it gives is nice context
so while radio dramas are technically speaking part of the story, they're not 100% necessary
wait
nefraea was in the **abyssal** prison
Cant really warp myself around excluding something like that from some stories
Some stories are short already why waste resources for something so unoptimal

Top 3 of my favorite Lovecraft stories

``` Randolph Carter dreams three times of a majestic sunset city, but each time he is abruptly snatched away before he can see it up close. When he prays to the gods of dream to reveal the whereabouts of the phantasmal city, they do not answer, and his dreams of the city stop altogether. Undaunted, Carter resolves to go to Kadath, where the gods live, to beseech them in person. ```

content: Honestly, what I find interesting about Hameln is how sort of... asexual his obsession with the keeper is. Like, even under the influence of the perverted tree, lol, he only plays music with them, and his feelings themselves don't seem to change that much (the whole thing about wanting to keep them asleep so he can protect them better is just as present in his regular convos)

hameln is so . . . he's such a character (positive) I adore him. I like that his obsession isn't necessarily romantic, makes my aro heart sing with joy. I love when characters are non-romantically obsessed with other characters, it's fun
